div1.entryFree:
Clerk, 1) scholar: great --s have purposed to greet me with premeditated welcomes, Mids. V, 93. large gifts have I bestowed on learned --s, H6B IV, 7, 76. all the --s, I mean the learned ones, H8 II, 2, 92. deep --s she dumbs, Per. V Prol. Per. V Prol.
2) the reader of responses in church service: and like unlettered c. still cry Amen, Sonn. 85, 6. answer, c. Ado II, 1, 114. Ado II, 1, 114 am I both priest and c.? well then, Amen, R2 IV, 173. take the priest, c., and some witnesses, Shr. IV, 4, 94.
3) a subordinate officer employed as a writer: c., draw a deed of gift, Merch. IV, 1, 394. the judge's c. V, 143. V, 143 V, 143 V, 143 V, 143 V, 143 V, 143 V, 143 the c. of Chatham, H6B IV, 2, 92.
4) St. Nicholas' --s (== highwaymen) H4A II, 1, 68; cf. Nicholas.
